---
title: "CV"
toc: true
---

## Education

- **PhD in Computer Science**, Georgia State University, 2020–present
  TReNDS Center (GSU, Georgia Tech, Emory). Advisor: Prof. Vince D. Calhoun

- **MS in Computer Science**, University of Georgia, 2017–2020

- **BS in Management**, Shandong University of Science and Technology, 2013–2017

## Research Experience

**Graduate Research Assistant**, TReNDS Center, 2020–present

- Developing multimodal deep learning frameworks for brain imaging analysis
- Investigating spectral methods for structural quality assessment
- Exploring theoretical connections between statistical physics, information theory, and neural systems

## Selected Publications

See full list on the [Publications](publications.qmd) page.

- **Bi Y**, Abrol A, Fu Z, Calhoun VD. A multimodal vision transformer for interpretable fusion of functional and structural neuroimaging data. *Human Brain Mapping*, 2024.
- **Bi Y**, Abrol A, Fu Z, Calhoun VD. Gray Matters: An efficient vision transformer GAN framework for predicting functional network connectivity biomarkers from brain structure. *bioRxiv*, 2024.
- **Bi Y**, Zhu Y, Calhoun VD. Redundancy as a structural information principle for learning and generalization. *arXiv*, 2025.

## Certifications

- **CFA Level III**, CFA Institute

## Skills

- **Programming**: Python, PyTorch, TensorFlow, MATLAB, R
- **Neuroimaging**: fMRI, sMRI, functional network connectivity analysis
- **Theory**: Statistical physics, information theory, spectral graph theory
- **Languages**: Chinese (native), English (professional)

## Links

- [Google Scholar](https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=yclnpogAAAAJ&hl=en)
- [GitHub](https://github.com/yudabitrends)
- [LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/in/yudabi/)
